title: Add base_branch as a first-class epic field (tk update --base-branch, shown in tk show)
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §3 base-branch + phasing row 4 (replaces the note convention from phase 3).

Changes:
1. internal/tick/tick.go: add BaseBranch string field, json:"base_branch,omitempty". Additive — absent for all existing ticks.
2. cmd/tk/cmd/update.go: --base-branch string flag (validate: non-empty implies type epic, error otherwise).
3. cmd/tk/cmd/show.go: display Base branch line for epics when set.
4. skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md: in the Resuming a run > Base branch subsection, make the field the PRIMARY mechanism (tk update <epic-id> --base-branch <name>, read via tk show <epic-id> --json .base_branch) and demote the tk note convention to a fallback line for older tk versions. Keep edits to that subsection only.

Consumer semantics: this field tells the orchestrator which branch to merge into, diff against, and resume on for THIS epic run. It is orchestrator-owned metadata, not a git operation — tk never touches git because of it.

Test cases: update sets + show displays + JSON round-trips; --base-branch on a task errors; unset field absent from JSON.

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes; tk update <epic> --base-branch works and tk show displays it; claude-runner.md base-branch subsection prefers the field with note fallback
closed_reason: base_branch first-class epic field merged (model+update+show+skill doc, 7 new tests)
